faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Doppler VPN

what.question: What is Doppler VPN?
what.answer: Doppler VPN is a privacy-focused virtual private network service that encrypts your internet connection and hides your IP address. It includes built-in ad blocking, content filtering by category, and support for custom blocklists - all without requiring registration.

noLogs.question: Is Doppler VPN really no-logs?
noLogs.answer: Yes, we operate a strict no-logs policy. We don't track, store, or share any of your browsing activity, connection timestamps, IP addresses, or any other personal data. Your privacy is our priority.

adBlocker.question: How does the ad blocker work?
adBlocker.answer: Our ad blocker works at the DNS level, blocking ads, trackers, and malicious domains before they even load. This means faster browsing, less data usage, and protection across all your apps - not just your browser.

categories.question: What content categories can I filter?
categories.answer: You can filter content across multiple categories including adult content, gambling, social media, gaming, streaming, and more. You can also import custom blocklists for additional content filtering control.

devices.question: How many devices can I use?
devices.answer: A single Doppler VPN subscription covers up to 10 devices simultaneously. Use it on your phones, tablets, and computers - all protected at once.

platforms.question: What platforms are supported?
platforms.answer: Doppler VPN is available on iOS (iPhone & iPad), macOS, and Android. We're constantly working on expanding our platform support.

cancel.question: How do I cancel my subscription?
cancel.answer: You can cancel your subscription anytime through your device's app store (App Store for iOS/macOS or Google Play for Android). Your access continues until the end of your billing period. You will not be charged after cancellation.

trial.question: Is there a free trial?
trial.answer: Yes! Every new user gets a 3-day free trial with full access to all Pro features. We also offer a 30-day money-back guarantee on all plans.

whatIsIncluded.question: What is included in Premium?
whatIsIncluded.answer: Premium includes VPN servers in 50+ locations with VLESS-Reality encryption, DNS-level ad blocker that works across all apps, content filter with parental controls, and connection on up to 10 devices simultaneously.

plans.question: What subscription plans are available?
plans.answer: We offer three plans: Monthly, 6-Month, and Yearly. All plans include the same Premium features. Longer plans offer a lower per-month price. Subscriptions auto-renew unless canceled at least 24 hours before the end of the billing period.

restore.question: I subscribed but my features aren't working
restore.answer: Open the app, go to Profile > Subscription and tap Restore Purchases. This will re-sync your subscription with our servers. If the issue persists, contact our support team at support@simnetiq.store or contact @DopplerSupportBot on Telegram.

refund.question: How do I request a refund?
refund.answer: We offer a 14-day refund for EU/UK customers and a 30-day goodwill money-back guarantee for all other customers. For web subscriptions (via Revolut), contact us at support@simnetiq.store. For iOS, visit reportaproblem.apple.com. For Android, open Google Play > Account > Order history. See our full Refund Policy at dopplervpn.org/refund for details.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy explains how SIMNETIQ LTD ("Company", "we", "us", or "our"), a company registered in England and Wales (Company Number 16861177), with its registered office at 2 Frederick Street, Kings Cross, London, WC1X 0ND, United Kingdom, collects, uses, and protects your information when you use DopplerVPN (our VPN application available on iOS, Android, and Windows) and our AI-powered video generation tool (collectively, the "Services"). We are committed to protecting your privacy in compliance with the UK General Data Protection Regulation (UK GDPR), the EU General Data Protection Regulation (EU GDPR), the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), and all other applicable data protection laws.

noLogs.title: 1. No-Logs Policy (VPN Service)
noLogs.content: DopplerVPN operates under a strict no-logs policy. We do not track, record, store, or share any of the following: your browsing activity or history, connection timestamps, originating or assigned IP addresses, DNS queries, bandwidth usage or transfer amounts, VPN session duration, or any other data that could be used to identify you or your online activities. All VPN traffic is encrypted end-to-end using the VLESS-Reality protocol and is never inspected, logged, modified, or stored on our servers. This policy applies to all users regardless of jurisdiction.

collect.title: 2. Information We Collect
collect.content: We collect only the minimum information necessary to provide and improve our Services. For DopplerVPN: a randomly generated device identifier (not linked to your personal identity), anonymous aggregated server performance metrics, and your email address only if you voluntarily contact support. Payment information: all payments are processed securely through Revolut (web subscriptions), Apple App Store (iOS), Google Play Store (Android), or RevenueCat (mobile subscription management). We never directly access, store, or process your credit card numbers, bank account details, or other financial information. For full details on how we process your data, see our Data Processing Agreement at dopplervpn.org/dpa.

vpnData.title: 3. VPN Data Handling
vpnData.content: When you connect to DopplerVPN, your internet traffic is encrypted using the VLESS-Reality protocol and routed through our servers. We do not inspect, log, store, modify, or sell any data that passes through our VPN tunnel. Our DNS-level ad blocker and content filter operate on our DNS resolver and do not log individual queries or browsing patterns. Our server infrastructure is configured to minimize data retention by design, and we do not have the technical ability to match a specific user to specific network activity.

legalBasis.title: 4. Legal Basis for Processing (GDPR)
legalBasis.content: Under the EU and UK GDPR, we process your personal data on the following legal bases: (a) Contract Performance — processing necessary to provide the Services you have subscribed to; (b) Legitimate Interest — processing necessary for our legitimate business interests, such as improving our Services and preventing fraud, provided these interests are not overridden by your rights; (c) Consent — where you have given explicit consent, such as subscribing to marketing communications; (d) Legal Obligation — processing necessary to comply with applicable laws. You may withdraw your consent at any time by contacting us at support@simnetiq.store.

rights.title: 5. Your Rights
rights.content: Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the following rights regarding your personal data: the right to access the personal data we hold about you, the right to rectification of inaccurate data, the right to erasure ("right to be forgotten"), the right to restrict processing, the right to data portability, the right to object to processing, and the right not to be subject to automated decision-making. EU/EEA and UK residents may exercise these rights under GDPR by contacting us at support@simnetiq.store. California residents may exercise their rights under the CCPA by the same contact method. We will respond to all legitimate requests within 30 days. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with your local data protection authority — for UK residents, this is the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO).

security.title: 6. Data Security
security.content: We implement industry-standard technical and organisational measures to protect your data, including: end-to-end encryption using the VLESS-Reality protocol for VPN traffic, TLS encryption for all web communications, regular security audits and infrastructure reviews, strict access controls on all production systems, and data minimisation by design across all Services. While no method of electronic transmission or storage is 100% secure, we take all reasonable steps to protect your personal information.

thirdParty.title: 7. Third-Party Services
thirdParty.content: We do not sell, trade, or rent your personal information to third parties. We share data only with the following categories of service providers, who are contractually bound to process data only as instructed: Revolut (payment processing — processes web subscription payments), Apple App Store and Google Play Store (process mobile app payments), RevenueCat (mobile subscription management — processes anonymous purchase identifiers), cloud infrastructure providers (server hosting — no access to user data), and analytics providers (aggregate, anonymised usage data only). We may disclose information if required by law, court order, or governmental authority, though our no-logs policy means we have minimal data to provide. For a complete list of our subprocessors, visit dopplervpn.org/subprocessors.

international.title: 8. International Data Transfers
international.content: Our servers are located in multiple countries to provide optimal VPN performance. When your data is transferred outside the UK or EEA, we ensure adequate safeguards are in place through Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) approved by the European Commission, adequacy decisions where applicable, or other legally recognised transfer mechanisms. Due to our no-logs policy, the VPN data that passes through our servers in any jurisdiction is encrypted and not stored.

retention.title: 9. Data Retention
retention.content: We retain personal data only for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes for which it was collected: account information is retained while your account is active and for up to 30 days after deletion to allow for account recovery, payment records are retained as required by applicable tax and accounting laws (typically 6 years in the UK), support correspondence is retained for up to 12 months after resolution, and VPN usage data is not retained at all (no-logs policy). You can request deletion of your account and data via our account deletion page at dopplervpn.org/delete-account, or by contacting support@simnetiq.store.

cookies.title: 10. Cookies and Tracking
cookies.content: Our website (dopplervpn.org) uses essential cookies required for the website to function properly and anonymous analytics cookies to understand website traffic in aggregate. We do not use advertising cookies or cross-site tracking technologies. You can control cookie preferences through your browser settings. Our VPN and AI applications do not use cookies.

children.title: 11. Children's Privacy
children.content: Our Services are not directed at children under the age of 16 (or 13 in jurisdictions where applicable). We do not knowingly collect personal information from children. DopplerVPN's content filtering feature includes parental control capabilities, which operate at the DNS level without collecting or storing personal data about users of any age. If we become aware that we have collected personal data from a child without parental consent, we will take steps to delete that information promptly.

changes.title: 12. Changes to This Policy
changes.content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices or applicable laws. We will notify you of material changes by posting the updated policy on our website with a revised "Last updated" date, and where required by law, by email. Your continued use of the Services after any changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

contact.title: 13. Contact Us
contact.content: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, wish to exercise your data protection rights, or have a complaint, please contact us: SIMNETIQ LTD, 2 Frederick Street, Kings Cross, London, WC1X 0ND, United Kingdom. Email: support@simnetiq.store. For data protection matters, you may also contact the UK Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) at ico.org.uk.

android.title: Android Setup
android.subtitle: Set up Doppler VPN on your Android device
android.step1Desc: Download the Doppler VPN APK directly from our website. No Play Store needed.
android.step2Desc: Open the app, create an account, and subscribe to a Pro plan. You can also subscribe via our Telegram bot @dopplercreatebot or on the website.
android.step3Desc: Pick a server location from the list. Doppler connects using the fast and secure VLESS-Reality protocol.
android.step4Desc: Tap Connect and allow VPN permission when prompted. You're now protected!
android.troubleshoot1: If connection fails, try a different server location.
android.troubleshoot2: Make sure Doppler VPN has battery optimization disabled in your Android settings.
android.troubleshoot3: For help, message @DopplerSupportBot on Telegram — our AI support is available 24/7.

ios.title: iOS Setup
ios.subtitle: Set up Doppler VPN on your iPhone or iPad
ios.step1Desc: Install Doppler VPN from the App Store. It's free to download.
ios.step2Desc: Open the app, create an account, and subscribe to a Pro plan via in-app purchase. You can also subscribe via our Telegram bot @dopplercreatebot or on the website.
ios.step3Desc: Pick a server location from the list. Doppler connects using the fast and secure VLESS-Reality protocol.
ios.step4Desc: Tap Connect and allow VPN permission when prompted. Your traffic is now encrypted and protected!
ios.troubleshoot1: If the VPN won't connect, try a different server location.
ios.troubleshoot2: Ensure Doppler VPN has permission to add VPN configurations in Settings > General > VPN.
ios.troubleshoot3: For help, message @DopplerSupportBot on Telegram — our AI support is available 24/7.

windows.title: Windows Setup
windows.subtitle: Set up Doppler VPN on your Windows PC
windows.step1Desc: Download v2rayN from GitHub. Extract the ZIP file and run v2rayN.exe.
windows.step2Desc: Open our Telegram bot @dopplercreatebot, tap "Connect VPN", choose a server, and you'll receive a VLESS config link.

metadata.title: Internet Censorship Guide — How VPNs Get Blocked & How to Stay Connected

hero.title: Your Guide to Internet Freedom
hero.subtitle: How governments detect and block VPNs, how Doppler defeats every method, and what you should do now to stay connected as restrictions escalate.

detection.title: How Governments Detect and Block VPNs
detection.intro: Internet censorship has evolved far beyond simple IP blocking. Countries like Russia, China, and Iran deploy sophisticated systems that use machine learning to identify and terminate VPN connections in real time. Here's exactly how they do it.

tspu.title: The Infrastructure: TSPU and DPI
tspu.p1: Russia deploys TSPU (Technical Systems for Threat Countermeasures) — specialized hardware installed directly on ISP network nodes. China uses the Great Firewall, Iran operates NIN. These systems perform Deep Packet Inspection (DPI) on every packet crossing the network.
tspu.p2: Traditional VPN protocols like OpenVPN, WireGuard, and IPSec have distinct packet structures. DPI identifies them instantly and drops the connection. Even "obfuscated" modes often fail because the underlying handshake pattern remains detectable.
tspu.p3: When basic signature matching isn't enough, these systems activate machine learning modules. They stop looking inside packets (everything is encrypted) and start analyzing how the traffic behaves.

method1.title: Method 1: TLS Client Fingerprinting
method1.description: Every TLS client — Chrome, Firefox, Safari — sends connection parameters in a specific order during the handshake. This creates a unique fingerprint. A VPN client pretending to be HTTPS traffic often sends these parameters in a different order than any real browser. DPI compares the fingerprint against a database of known browsers. Mismatch? Flagged as a tunnel.

method2.title: Method 2: Traffic Entropy Analysis
method2.description: Real internet usage has distinctive patterns. Video streaming produces many large sequential packets. Web browsing follows a request-response-pause cycle. A VPN tunnel has a completely different entropy profile — uniform packet sizes, consistent timing, non-human density. Even when traffic is disguised as HTTPS, machine learning models detect these statistical anomalies in packet lengths and time delays.

method3.title: Method 3: Active Probing
method3.description: When the system suspects a foreign server is running a VPN, it sends its own probe request. If the server responds like a normal website — returning a 404 error or an HTML page — it passes. But if the server attempts to initiate a VPN handshake or behaves unexpectedly, the IP address is immediately banned. Simple VPNs fail this test instantly.

method4.title: Method 4: IP and DNS Blacklisting
method4.description: Known VPN provider IP ranges are blocked outright. DNS requests for VPN domains are intercepted. Commercial VPN providers with public server lists are the easiest targets — their entire infrastructure can be blocked in minutes.

detection.conclusion: The result: push-button VPNs and standard proxy services like NordVPN, ExpressVPN, and Surfshark stop working within hours of a crackdown. Only proxy protocols specifically engineered to be indistinguishable from regular web traffic survive.

defeats.title: How Doppler Defeats Every Detection Method
defeats.subtitle: Each detection technique has a specific countermeasure built into VLESS-Reality — the protocol that powers every Doppler connection.

signatures.description: VLESS-Reality produces no identifiable protocol pattern. There is no VPN handshake, no distinctive packet structure, nothing for DPI signature databases to match against.

fingerprinting.description: Reality performs a real TLS handshake using a legitimate website's certificate. The fingerprint matches exactly what a real browser would produce — because it is a real TLS connection.

entropy.description: Traffic flowing through a VLESS-Reality tunnel is formatted identically to standard HTTPS. Packet sizes, timing, and flow patterns match normal web browsing, defeating statistical analysis.

probing.description: When probed, Doppler servers respond exactly as the legitimate website they mirror. Unauthorized probe requests receive normal web responses. The VPN tunnel only activates with valid client authentication.

blacklisting.description: Doppler rotates server endpoints and IP addresses. Even if one endpoint is identified, the network adapts automatically. Our Telegram bot provides instant access to updated server configurations.

blocked.title: Blocked in Russia — And Still Here
blocked.p1: Russian authorities have specifically targeted and blocked Doppler VPN. Our domain has been added to the federal blocklist. This isn't something we hide — it's proof that our technology works.
blocked.p2: Unlike commercial VPN providers that comply with takedown requests or quietly exit restricted markets, Doppler was built for this exact scenario. When our website gets blocked, our Telegram bot remains accessible. When server IPs get banned, our infrastructure rotates automatically.
blocked.p3: Every block they impose, we bypass. That's not a marketing claim — it's our daily operation.

future.title: What to Expect: The Future of Internet Restrictions
future.intro: Internet censorship isn't standing still. Understanding where things are headed helps you prepare. Here's the realistic progression based on current infrastructure and policy trends.

stage1.label: Current Stage
stage1.title: DPI + Machine Learning Detection
stage1.description: Governments deploy TSPU/DPI hardware with ML modules on ISP nodes. Standard VPN protocols are blocked automatically. VLESS-Reality and similar anti-censorship protocols still work because they produce traffic indistinguishable from HTTPS.
stage1.status: Active now in Russia, China, Iran

stage2.label: Possible Next Step
stage2.title: Whitelist Model
stage2.description: Instead of blocking specific protocols, only approved domains and IP addresses would be accessible. Everything else is blocked by default. SSH, RDP, gaming ports — all blocked unless registered by a legal entity. This was partially tested during Russia's 2018 Telegram ban.

stage3.label: Extreme Scenario
stage3.title: National Internet Isolation
stage3.description: A fully isolated domestic network with mandatory state-issued root certificates, local mirrors of foreign services, and no direct access to the global internet. Think North Korea's Kwangmyong, but at scale.

reality.title: The Economic Reality Check
reality.p1: In 2018, when Roskomnadzor tried to block just Telegram, they ended up banning millions of Amazon and Google IP addresses. Cash registers in stores stopped working. Smart home devices went offline. Banking push notifications failed.
reality.p2: A whitelist model would cause this at 100x scale. Modern Russian websites depend on Google Fonts, foreign CDN libraries, international certificate authorities, and cloud services. Banks and logistics companies use foreign SSL certificates. Developers would lose access to GitHub, Docker Hub, and npm.
reality.p3: Full isolation is technically possible — but its price is technological degradation. Windows and Android updates become unavailable. Medical equipment loses driver support. AI development stops entirely without global open-source infrastructure.
reality.p4: The most likely path is gradual restriction over years, not a sudden cutoff. But each escalation step means more VPN users get left behind — unless they're prepared.

whyRegister.title: Why Most VPNs Require Registration
whyRegister.intro: Ever wondered why almost every VPN service asks for your email, phone number, or creates a mandatory account? The answer has less to do with security and more to do with business models built around your personal data.

dataHarvesting.title: Data Harvesting
dataHarvesting.description: When you register with an email address, VPN providers link your browsing activity to your identity. This data profile — websites visited, connection times, bandwidth usage — becomes a valuable asset. Some providers sell anonymized (or not-so-anonymized) usage data to third-party analytics firms and data brokers, turning your privacy tool into a surveillance product.

targetedAds.title: Targeted Advertising
targetedAds.description: Your registration email connects your VPN usage to advertising networks. Many free VPN services monetize through targeted ads, using your browsing patterns to serve personalized advertisements. Even paid VPNs with accounts can cross-reference your data with ad platforms to generate additional revenue streams.

govRequests.title: Government Data Requests
govRequests.description: When a VPN provider has your email and account tied to connection logs, they have something to hand over when law enforcement comes knocking. Subpoenas, national security letters, and court orders can compel providers to reveal exactly who was connected to what server at what time — connecting your real identity to your online activity.

breachLiability.title: Breach Liability
breachLiability.description: Every account database is a breach waiting to happen. VPN providers that store emails, passwords, and payment details create honeypots for hackers. When breaches occur — and they do regularly — your identity is exposed alongside the fact that you use a VPN, which in some regions can itself attract unwanted attention from authorities.

whyRegister.conclusion: The pattern is clear: registration exists to serve the provider, not the user. A truly private VPN should never need to know who you are.

howItWorks.title: How Doppler Works Without an Account
howItWorks.subtitle: Doppler replaces the traditional email-and-password model with device-based authentication. No registration, no sign up, no personal information of any kind.

download.description: Install Doppler VPN from the App Store, Google Play, or get a config through our Telegram bot. No account creation screen, no email field, no verification codes — the app is ready the moment it finishes installing.

generateKey.description: On first launch, Doppler generates a cryptographic device key locally on your phone or computer. This key is a random identifier — it contains no personal information, no hardware fingerprints, and cannot be traced back to you. Think of it like a random hotel room key that opens a door but carries no name.

connect.title: Connect to Any Server
connect.description: Your device key authenticates you with our VLESS-Reality servers. The connection is established using the same TLS 1.3 handshake that powers regular HTTPS websites, making your VPN traffic indistinguishable from normal web browsing. No login prompt, no credentials transmitted.

done.title: You're Protected
done.description: That's it. No email confirmation to wait for, no passwords to remember, no accounts to manage. Your connection is encrypted, your identity is anonymous, and your VPN has zero knowledge of who you are. You are browsing as a truly anonymous VPN user — no email required, no trace left behind.

noCollect.title: What We Don't Collect

deviceKey.title: Your Data, Your Device

deviceKey.p3: This approach mirrors how Apple's Private Relay works: your traffic is authenticated without being identified. The difference is that Doppler applies this principle to your entire internet connection, not just Safari browsing. Every app, every protocol, every packet — all protected without requiring a single piece of personal information.
deviceKey.p4: The result: even if our servers were seized, there would be no user accounts to expose, no emails to leak, no browsing histories to reveal. Your privacy is guaranteed by architecture, not by promises.

zeroPii.title: Zero PII Stored

metadata.description: Learn what the VLESS protocol is, how VLESS-Reality defeats Deep Packet Inspection, and why it outperforms WireGuard and OpenVPN for censorship bypass. Download the Doppler VLESS VPN app for iOS and Android.

hero.title: VLESS-Reality: The Protocol Built to Be Invisible
hero.subtitle: The next generation VPN protocol that defeats Deep Packet Inspection. VLESS-Reality makes your traffic indistinguishable from normal HTTPS — no signatures, no fingerprints, no detection.

whatIs.title: What Is the VLESS Protocol?
whatIs.intro: VLESS is a lightweight proxy protocol born from the V2Ray project — an open-source ecosystem built specifically to circumvent internet censorship. Understanding what VLESS is and how it evolved explains why it succeeds where traditional VPN protocols fail.

origin.title: From VMess to VLESS: The Evolution
origin.p1: The V2Ray project started in 2015 as a response to China's Great Firewall. Its original protocol, VMess, introduced traffic obfuscation — disguising proxy connections as regular web traffic. VMess worked well initially, but as DPI systems grew more sophisticated, its weaknesses became apparent: unnecessary complexity, encryption overhead, and identifiable handshake patterns.
origin.p2: VLESS (V2Ray-Less Encryption Serialization Stream) was created in 2020 as VMess's successor. The designers stripped away everything unnecessary: redundant encryption layers, complex authentication schemes, and protocol-specific markers. What remained was a minimal, stateless protocol with zero overhead — a clean transport layer designed for one purpose.
origin.p3: In 2022, the XTLS project introduced Reality — a TLS camouflage layer that fundamentally changed what was possible. VLESS-Reality doesn't just obfuscate traffic. It borrows the TLS certificate of a legitimate website, making the connection genuinely indistinguishable from normal HTTPS browsing to any observer, including state-level DPI systems.

difference.title: What Makes VLESS Different
difference.p1: Traditional VPN protocols like WireGuard and OpenVPN prioritize speed and encryption. They create encrypted tunnels, but those tunnels have distinctive signatures — recognizable packet structures, handshake patterns, and traffic profiles. A DPI system identifies them within seconds. VLESS takes the opposite approach: invisibility first. Instead of building an encrypted tunnel that looks like a tunnel, VLESS-Reality creates a connection that looks exactly like you browsing a normal website.
difference.p2: This is the core difference: VLESS-Reality is the only protocol where the connection itself is indistinguishable from regular HTTPS traffic — not obfuscated, not disguised, but genuinely identical at the packet level.

howItWorks.title: How VLESS-Reality Works
howItWorks.subtitle: A four-step process that makes your VPN connection invisible to Deep Packet Inspection, active probing, and traffic analysis — explained without the jargon.

step1.title: TLS Handshake Mimicry
step1.description: When you connect, VLESS-Reality initiates a real TLS 1.3 handshake with a legitimate website — like google.com or microsoft.com. From the outside, this looks exactly like your browser connecting to that website. The DPI system sees a standard, expected connection and lets it through.

step2.title: Certificate Borrowing
step2.description: Unlike traditional VPNs that use self-signed certificates (a red flag for DPI), Reality borrows the TLS certificate of the target website. The certificate chain is genuine. Any system inspecting the connection sees a valid certificate from a trusted authority — because it is one.

step3.title: Traffic Camouflage
step3.description: Your actual VPN data is multiplexed within the established TLS connection. Packet sizes, timing intervals, and traffic patterns are shaped to match what normal HTTPS browsing looks like. Machine learning models trained to detect VPN entropy patterns see nothing abnormal.

step4.title: Reverse Proxy Behavior
step4.description: If anyone probes the server — censors, automated scanners, or DPI active probing systems — the server responds exactly like the legitimate website it's mimicking. It serves real web pages, returns proper error codes, and behaves indistinguishably from the real site. The VPN functionality is completely hidden.

howItWorks.conclusion: The result: your connection is not just encrypted — it's invisible. No DPI system, machine learning model, or active probe can distinguish it from millions of legitimate HTTPS connections happening every second.

comparison.title: VLESS vs WireGuard vs OpenVPN — Protocol Comparison
comparison.subtitle: How does VLESS-Reality stack up against other VPN protocols? This comparison covers the metrics that matter for users in censored regions.

whyNot.title: Why Consumer VPNs Don't Use VLESS
whyNot.subtitle: If VLESS-Reality is so effective, why don't NordVPN, ExpressVPN, or Surfshark use it? The answer reveals why most commercial VPNs are built for the wrong problem.

card1.title: Implementation Complexity
card1.description: VLESS-Reality requires Xray-core — a specialized proxy engine with complex server-side configuration. Each server needs individual TLS certificate targets, SNI configuration, and Reality key pairs. Commercial VPNs run thousands of servers and optimize for simplicity, not stealth. Deploying VLESS-Reality at scale requires deep protocol expertise that most VPN companies simply don't have.

card2.title: Higher Server Costs
card2.description: VLESS-Reality servers perform real TLS handshakes with target websites, proxy traffic through multiple layers, and must respond convincingly to active probes. This consumes significantly more CPU and bandwidth than a simple WireGuard tunnel. For VPN companies optimizing margins across thousands of servers, the cost increase is prohibitive.

card3.title: No Consumer Packaging
card3.description: Until recently, using VLESS-Reality meant manually configuring Xray-core clients, generating UUID keys, and editing JSON config files. There was no consumer-friendly app — no one-tap connect, no automatic server selection. The protocol existed only as a technical tool for advanced users, not as a product.

card4.title: Wrong Optimization Target
card4.description: Most commercial VPNs are built for streaming access and privacy marketing — not censorship bypass. WireGuard is faster for Netflix unblocking. OpenVPN is easier to deploy. These VPNs have no incentive to solve the censorship problem because their customers are mostly in countries without internet restrictions.

whyNot.conclusion: Doppler solves all four problems: Xray-core expertise, optimized infrastructure, consumer-grade apps, and a focus on users who actually need censorship bypass.

performance.title: Real-World Performance
performance.p1: VLESS-Reality is not just invisible — it's fast. Because the protocol adds minimal overhead (no double encryption, no unnecessary handshake layers), actual throughput approaches raw connection speed. Users in Russia, Iran, and China consistently report reliable connections even during active crackdowns.
performance.p2: These metrics are from Doppler's production infrastructure running VLESS-Reality across multiple server regions, measured during periods of active DPI enforcement.

hero.subtitle: Fast, private, and censorship-resistant VPN built for Windows. No registration required — download the installer and connect in seconds. Supports both x64 and ARM64 PCs with automatic updates.

features.title: Why Choose Doppler VPN for Windows?
features.subtitle: Doppler VPN is a native Windows application designed for PC users who need real privacy, censorship bypass, and reliable performance on any Windows device.

nativeWindows.title: Native Windows App
nativeWindows.description: Built specifically for Windows with a clean, modern interface. Runs on Windows 10 and Windows 11 with full support for both x64 and ARM64 architectures. Installs in seconds with a simple setup wizard — no manual configuration or command-line tools needed.

dualArchitecture.title: x64 + ARM64 Support
dualArchitecture.description: Doppler VPN provides dedicated builds for both x64 (standard PCs, desktops, and most laptops) and ARM64 (Surface Pro X, Snapdragon laptops). Choose the right installer for your hardware and get native performance on any Windows device.

autoUpdates.title: Automatic Updates via Velopack
autoUpdates.description: Doppler VPN uses Velopack for seamless background updates. New versions are downloaded and applied automatically — you always have the latest security patches, protocol improvements, and features without lifting a finger.

vlessEncryption.title: VLESS-Reality Encryption
vlessEncryption.description: Next-generation protocol that disguises VPN traffic as regular HTTPS browsing. Your connection is invisible to firewalls, deep packet inspection systems, and ISP monitoring tools. The same technology used to bypass the Great Firewall of China.

noRegistration.title: No Registration Required
noRegistration.description: No email, no phone number, no account creation. Just install the app and connect instantly. Doppler uses device-based authentication so your identity stays completely private from the very first click.

bypassCensorship.title: Bypass Censorship & DPI
bypassCensorship.description: Designed to work in countries with strict internet censorship — Russia, Iran, China, and beyond. VLESS-Reality makes your VPN traffic indistinguishable from normal web browsing, defeating TSPU and GFW.

noLogs.title: Strict No-Logs Policy
noLogs.description: Doppler never collects, stores, or shares your browsing data. No connection logs, no traffic logs, no DNS query logs. We cannot identify you because we never collect identifying information.

globalServers.title: 6 Global Server Locations
globalServers.description: Connect to servers in multiple countries across Europe and beyond. Each server runs VLESS-Reality with optimized routing for the lowest latency and highest throughput. Automatic server selection picks the fastest option for your location.

howItWorks.title: How to Set Up VPN on Windows
howItWorks.subtitle: Get connected in under a minute — no technical knowledge required.

step1.title: Download the Installer
step1.description: Download the Doppler VPN installer for your system — x64 for most PCs and laptops, ARM64 for Surface Pro X and Snapdragon devices. The installer is under 30 MB and virus-free.

step2.title: Run the Setup Wizard
step2.description: Double-click the installer and follow the setup wizard. Installation takes about 15 seconds. Doppler VPN is added to your Start menu and can optionally launch at Windows startup.

step3.title: Open Doppler VPN
step3.description: Launch the app from your Start menu or desktop shortcut. There is no sign-up screen, no registration form, and no email verification. Your account is created automatically using device-based authentication.

step4.title: Subscribe and Connect
step4.description: Choose a subscription plan and click Connect. Doppler automatically selects the best server, establishes an encrypted VLESS-Reality tunnel, and routes all your traffic through it. Your IP is hidden and your data is encrypted.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions — Doppler VPN for Windows

q1.question: What Windows versions are supported?
q1.answer: Doppler VPN supports Windows 10 (version 1809 and later) and Windows 11. Both x64 and ARM64 architectures are supported. The app works on desktops, laptops, tablets, and Surface devices running compatible Windows versions.

q2.question: What's the difference between x64 and ARM64?
q2.answer: x64 is for most standard PCs, desktops, and laptops with Intel or AMD processors. ARM64 is for newer devices like Microsoft Surface Pro X, Lenovo ThinkPad X13s, and other Snapdragon-powered Windows laptops. If you're unsure, download the x64 version — it works on the majority of Windows computers.

q3.question: Does Doppler VPN auto-update on Windows?
q3.answer: Yes. Doppler VPN uses Velopack for automatic background updates. When a new version is available, it downloads in the background and applies the update the next time you launch the app. You always have the latest security patches and features without any manual action.

q4.question: Can I use the same account on my phone and PC?
q4.answer: Yes. Your Doppler VPN subscription works across all your devices — Windows, iOS, Android, and Mac. Each device gets its own secure VPN connection. Up to 10 devices can be connected to a single account.

q5.question: Does it work with Windows Defender?
q5.answer: Yes. Doppler VPN is fully compatible with Windows Defender and other major antivirus software. The installer is digitally signed and the app does not trigger false positives. Doppler operates as a standard VPN client using Windows networking APIs.

q6.question: Does it work in Russia, China, and Iran?
q6.answer: Yes. Doppler uses the VLESS-Reality protocol, which disguises VPN traffic as regular HTTPS web browsing. This makes it extremely difficult for censorship systems like Russia's TSPU, China's Great Firewall, or Iran's filtering infrastructure to detect and block. Doppler is specifically designed for users in heavily censored regions.

dpa.title: Data Processing Agreement
dpa.intro: This Data Processing Agreement ("DPA") forms part of the agreement between you and SIMNETIQ LTD (Company Number 16861177), registered at 2 Frederick Street, Kings Cross, London, WC1X 0ND, United Kingdom, governing the processing of personal data in connection with Doppler VPN services.

scope.title: 1. Scope & Definitions
scope.content: This DPA applies to all processing of personal data carried out by SIMNETIQ LTD (the "Controller") and any processors engaged to deliver Doppler VPN services. "Data Subjects" means users of Doppler VPN services. "Personal Data" means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person, as defined under the UK GDPR and EU GDPR. "Processor" means any entity that processes personal data on behalf of the Controller.

purpose.title: 2. Processing Purpose & Instructions
purpose.content: Personal data is processed solely for the purposes of: VPN service provision and infrastructure management, user account creation and management, payment processing and subscription management, customer support and communication, and service improvement through anonymised analytics. All processing is carried out in accordance with the Controller's documented instructions and applicable data protection law.

roles.title: 3. Data Controller & Processor Roles
roles.content: SIMNETIQ LTD acts as the Data Controller, determining the purposes and means of processing personal data. Third-party service providers engaged by SIMNETIQ LTD act as Data Processors and are bound by contractual obligations equivalent to those set out in this DPA.

dataTypes.title: 4. Types of Personal Data Processed
dataTypes.content: The following categories of personal data may be processed: account identifiers (randomly generated VPN-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX format), email addresses (when voluntarily provided for account recovery or support), device identifiers (anonymous, not linked to personal identity), payment records (processed by third-party payment providers — Revolut, Apple, Google), and support correspondence (email communications with our support team). We do not process sensitive or special category data.

security.title: 5. Security Measures
security.content: We implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure the security of personal data, including: VLESS-Reality encryption for all VPN traffic, TLS encryption for all web communications, strict access controls on production systems with principle of least privilege, regular security audits and infrastructure reviews, data minimisation by design across all services, and a strict no-logs policy for VPN usage — we do not store, inspect, or retain any data passing through our VPN tunnel.

subprocessors.title: 6. Sub-processor Obligations
subprocessors.content: All sub-processors are bound by data processing terms equivalent to those in this DPA. A current list of sub-processors is maintained at dopplervpn.org/subprocessors. We will provide at least 30 days' notice before engaging any new sub-processor. Data Subjects may object to the appointment of a new sub-processor by contacting support@simnetiq.store within the notice period.

rights.title: 7. Data Subject Rights
rights.content: Data Subjects have the right to: access their personal data held by the Controller, rectification of inaccurate personal data, erasure of personal data ("right to be forgotten"), data portability in a structured, commonly used format, restriction of processing, objection to processing based on legitimate interests. To exercise any of these rights, contact support@simnetiq.store. We will respond to all requests within 30 days.

breach.title: 8. Data Breach Notification
breach.content: In the event of a personal data breach, the Controller will: notify the relevant supervisory authority within 72 hours of becoming aware of the breach (where required by law), notify affected Data Subjects without undue delay where the breach is likely to result in a high risk to their rights and freedoms, document the breach including its effects and remedial actions taken, and cooperate with supervisory authorities as required.

retention.title: 9. Data Retention
retention.content: Personal data is retained only as long as necessary for the purposes stated in this DPA: account information is retained while the account is active and for 30 days after deletion to allow for recovery, payment records are retained for 6 years as required by UK tax and accounting law, support correspondence is retained for 12 months after resolution, and VPN usage data is NOT retained — our no-logs policy means we do not have the technical ability to associate specific network activity with individual users.

transfers.title: 10. International Data Transfers
transfers.content: Where personal data is transferred outside the UK or EEA, we ensure adequate safeguards through: Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) approved by the European Commission, UK adequacy decisions where applicable, and other legally recognised transfer mechanisms. Our VPN infrastructure spans multiple countries; however, due to our no-logs policy, VPN traffic data is encrypted and not stored in any jurisdiction.

audit.title: 11. Audit Rights
audit.content: Data Subjects may request information about how their personal data is processed by contacting support@simnetiq.store. SIMNETIQ LTD conducts an annual compliance review of its data processing activities and sub-processor arrangements to ensure ongoing compliance with applicable data protection laws.

termination.title: 12. Term & Termination
termination.content: This DPA is effective for as long as the Data Subject uses Doppler VPN services. Upon termination of service use, personal data will be deleted in accordance with the retention periods specified in Section 9. Data that is no longer needed will be securely erased. Data required to be retained by law will be securely stored for the applicable retention period and then deleted.

subprocessors.title: Subprocessors
subprocessors.intro: The following third-party service providers (subprocessors) process personal data on behalf of SIMNETIQ LTD in connection with Doppler VPN services. Each subprocessor is bound by data processing terms equivalent to our Data Processing Agreement.
subprocessors.notice: We notify users at least 30 days before adding new subprocessors. If you have questions about our subprocessors, contact support@simnetiq.store.
